title: Holandrea caucasica
taxonomy: Holandrea caucasica is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Holandrea. This plant is native to the Caucasus region. The classification of Holandrea caucasica includes its kingdom as Plantae and its order as Lamiales. Further classification also places it in the family Lamiaceae.

intro: Holandrea caucasica, also known as Caucasian holly, is a plant species native to the Caucasus region, characterized by its fragrant leaves and bright yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Holandrea caucasica include essential oils, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Holandrea caucasica is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
